# Code automatically generated from OptiMUS

# Problem type: LP        
# Problem description
'''
A clinical firm operates two factories, northern and western. The firm decides
the number of hours to run each factory. The northern factory produces
NorthernFactoryAntiItchRate grams of anti-itch injections and
NorthernFactoryTopicalCreamRate grams of topical cream per hour. The western
factory produces WesternFactoryAntiItchRate grams of anti-itch injections and
WesternFactoryTopicalCreamRate grams of topical cream per hour. The northern
factory uses NorthernFactoryPlasticUsage units of plastic per hour, and the
western factory uses WesternFactoryPlasticUsage units of plastic per hour. The
total plastic available is TotalPlasticAvailable units. The firm must produce at
least MinimumAntiItchProduction grams of anti-itch injections and
MinimumTopicalCreamProduction grams of topical cream. The objective is to
minimize the total time the factories are run.
'''
# Import necessary libraries
import json
from gurobipy import *
     
# Create a new model
model = Model()

# Load data 
with open("/Users/gaowenzhi/Desktop/optimus-OR-paper/data/new_dataset/sample_datasets/261/parameters.json", "r") as f:
    data = json.load(f)
    
# @Def: definition of a target
# @Shape: shape of a target
        
# Parameters 
# @Parameter NorthernFactoryAntiItchRate @Def: Production rate of anti-itch injections per hour at the northern factory @Shape: [] 
NorthernFactoryAntiItchRate = data['NorthernFactoryAntiItchRate']
# @Parameter NorthernFactoryTopicalCreamRate @Def: Production rate of topical cream per hour at the northern factory @Shape: [] 
NorthernFactoryTopicalCreamRate = data['NorthernFactoryTopicalCreamRate']
# @Parameter WesternFactoryAntiItchRate @Def: Production rate of anti-itch injections per hour at the western factory @Shape: [] 
WesternFactoryAntiItchRate = data['WesternFactoryAntiItchRate']
# @Parameter WesternFactoryTopicalCreamRate @Def: Production rate of topical cream per hour at the western factory @Shape: [] 
WesternFactoryTopicalCreamRate = data['WesternFactoryTopicalCreamRate']
# @Parameter NorthernFactoryPlasticUsage @Def: Units of plastic required per hour at the northern factory @Shape: [] 
NorthernFactoryPlasticUsage = data['NorthernFactoryPlasticUsage']
# @Parameter WesternFactoryPlasticUsage @Def: Units of plastic required per hour at the western factory @Shape: [] 
WesternFactoryPlasticUsage = data['WesternFactoryPlasticUsage']
# @Parameter TotalPlasticAvailable @Def: Total units of plastic available @Shape: [] 
TotalPlasticAvailable = data['TotalPlasticAvailable']
# @Parameter MinimumAntiItchProduction @Def: Minimum grams of anti-itch injections to produce @Shape: [] 
MinimumAntiItchProduction = data['MinimumAntiItchProduction']
# @Parameter MinimumTopicalCreamProduction @Def: Minimum grams of topical cream to produce @Shape: [] 
MinimumTopicalCreamProduction = data['MinimumTopicalCreamProduction']

# Variables 
# @Variable NorthernFactoryAntiItchProduction @Def: The production quantity of anti-itch injections at the Northern factory @Shape: [] 
NorthernFactoryAntiItchProduction = model.addVar(vtype=GRB.CONTINUOUS, name="NorthernFactoryAntiItchProduction")
# @Variable NorthernFactoryTopicalCreamProduction @Def: The production quantity of topical cream at the Northern factory @Shape: [] 
NorthernFactoryTopicalCreamProduction = model.addVar(vtype=GRB.CONTINUOUS, name="NorthernFactoryTopicalCreamProduction")
# @Variable WesternFactoryAntiItchProduction @Def: The production quantity of anti-itch injections at the Western factory @Shape: [] 
WesternFactoryAntiItchProduction = model.addVar(vtype=GRB.CONTINUOUS, name="WesternFactoryAntiItchProduction")
# @Variable WesternFactoryTopicalCreamProduction @Def: The production quantity of topical cream at the Western factory @Shape: [] 
WesternFactoryTopicalCreamProduction = model.addVar(lb=0, vtype=GRB.CONTINUOUS, name="WesternFactoryTopicalCreamProduction")
# @Variable NorthernRunTime @Def: The run time of the Northern factory in hours @Shape: [] 
NorthernRunTime = model.addVar(vtype=GRB.CONTINUOUS, name="NorthernRunTime")
# @Variable WesternRunTime @Def: The run time of the Western factory in hours @Shape: [] 
WesternRunTime = model.addVar(vtype=GRB.CONTINUOUS, name="WesternRunTime")

# Constraints 
# @Constraint Constr_1 @Def: The total plastic used by the northern and western factories cannot exceed TotalPlasticAvailable units.
model.addConstr((NorthernFactoryPlasticUsage / NorthernFactoryAntiItchRate) * NorthernFactoryAntiItchProduction + (NorthernFactoryPlasticUsage / NorthernFactoryTopicalCreamRate) * NorthernFactoryTopicalCreamProduction + (WesternFactoryPlasticUsage / WesternFactoryAntiItchRate) * WesternFactoryAntiItchProduction + (WesternFactoryPlasticUsage / WesternFactoryTopicalCreamRate) * WesternFactoryTopicalCreamProduction <= TotalPlasticAvailable)
# @Constraint Constr_2 @Def: The production of anti-itch injections by the northern and western factories must be at least MinimumAntiItchProduction grams.
model.addConstr(NorthernFactoryAntiItchProduction + WesternFactoryAntiItchProduction >= MinimumAntiItchProduction)
# @Constraint Constr_3 @Def: The production of topical cream by the northern and western factories must be at least MinimumTopicalCreamProduction grams.
model.addConstr(NorthernFactoryTopicalCreamProduction + WesternFactoryTopicalCreamProduction >= MinimumTopicalCreamProduction)

# Objective 
# @Objective Objective @Def: The objective is to minimize the total time the northern and western factories are run.
model.setObjective(NorthernRunTime + WesternRunTime, GRB.MINIMIZE)

# Solve 
model.optimize()

# Extract solution 
solution = {}
variables = {}
objective = []
variables['NorthernFactoryAntiItchProduction'] = NorthernFactoryAntiItchProduction.x
variables['NorthernFactoryTopicalCreamProduction'] = NorthernFactoryTopicalCreamProduction.x
variables['WesternFactoryAntiItchProduction'] = WesternFactoryAntiItchProduction.x
variables['WesternFactoryTopicalCreamProduction'] = WesternFactoryTopicalCreamProduction.x
variables['NorthernRunTime'] = NorthernRunTime.x
variables['WesternRunTime'] = WesternRunTime.x
solution['variables'] = variables
solution['objective'] = model.objVal
with open('solution.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(solution, f, indent=4)

# Code automatically generated from OptiMUS

# Problem type: MIP        
# Problem description
'''
A hospital prepares two types of medication: medication patches and anti-biotic
creams. Each batch of medication patches requires PreparationTimeMedicationPatch
minutes to prepare and MaterialRequiredMedicationPatch units of materials. Each
batch of anti-biotic creams requires PreparationTimeAntiBioticCream minutes to
prepare and MaterialRequiredAntiBioticCream units of materials. Since anti-
biotic creams are used more often, the number of batches of anti-biotic creams
must be at least MinRatioAntiBioticCreamToMedicationPatch times the number of
batches of medication patches. Due to storage constraints, the total number of
batches of medication patches and anti-biotic creams cannot exceed
MaxTotalBatches. The hospital has AvailableStaffTime minutes of staff time
available and AvailableMaterials units of materials. Each batch of medication
patches can treat TreatmentPerBatchMedicationPatch people, and each batch of
anti-biotic creams can treat TreatmentPerBatchAntiBioticCream people. The
hospital aims to determine the number of batches of each product to maximize the
number of people that can be treated.
'''
# Import necessary libraries
import json
from gurobipy import *
     
# Create a new model
model = Model()

# Load data 
with open("/Users/gaowenzhi/Desktop/optimus-OR-paper/data/new_dataset/sample_datasets/262/parameters.json", "r") as f:
    data = json.load(f)
    
# @Def: definition of a target
# @Shape: shape of a target
        
# Parameters 
# @Parameter PreparationTimeMedicationPatch @Def: Preparation time per batch of medication patches @Shape: [] 
PreparationTimeMedicationPatch = data['PreparationTimeMedicationPatch']
# @Parameter PreparationTimeAntiBioticCream @Def: Preparation time per batch of anti-biotic creams @Shape: [] 
PreparationTimeAntiBioticCream = data['PreparationTimeAntiBioticCream']
# @Parameter MaterialRequiredMedicationPatch @Def: Material required per batch of medication patches @Shape: [] 
MaterialRequiredMedicationPatch = data['MaterialRequiredMedicationPatch']
# @Parameter MaterialRequiredAntiBioticCream @Def: Material required per batch of anti-biotic creams @Shape: [] 
MaterialRequiredAntiBioticCream = data['MaterialRequiredAntiBioticCream']
# @Parameter MinRatioAntiBioticCreamToMedicationPatch @Def: Minimum ratio of anti-biotic creams to medication patches @Shape: [] 
MinRatioAntiBioticCreamToMedicationPatch = data['MinRatioAntiBioticCreamToMedicationPatch']
# @Parameter MaxTotalBatches @Def: Maximum total number of batches @Shape: [] 
MaxTotalBatches = data['MaxTotalBatches']
# @Parameter AvailableStaffTime @Def: Available staff time in minutes @Shape: [] 
AvailableStaffTime = data['AvailableStaffTime']
# @Parameter AvailableMaterials @Def: Available units of materials @Shape: [] 
AvailableMaterials = data['AvailableMaterials']
# @Parameter TreatmentPerBatchMedicationPatch @Def: Number of people treated by each batch of medication patches @Shape: [] 
TreatmentPerBatchMedicationPatch = data['TreatmentPerBatchMedicationPatch']
# @Parameter TreatmentPerBatchAntiBioticCream @Def: Number of people treated by each batch of anti-biotic creams @Shape: [] 
TreatmentPerBatchAntiBioticCream = data['TreatmentPerBatchAntiBioticCream']

# Variables 
# @Variable NumBatchesAntiBioticCream @Def: The number of batches of anti-biotic creams @Shape: [] 
NumBatchesAntiBioticCream = model.addVar(vtype=GRB.INTEGER, name="NumBatchesAntiBioticCream")
# @Variable NumBatchesMedicationPatch @Def: The number of batches of medication patches @Shape: [] 
NumBatchesMedicationPatch = model.addVar(vtype=GRB.INTEGER, name="NumBatchesMedicationPatch")

# Constraints 
# @Constraint Constr_1 @Def: The number of batches of anti-biotic creams must be at least MinRatioAntiBioticCreamToMedicationPatch times the number of batches of medication patches.
model.addConstr(NumBatchesAntiBioticCream >= MinRatioAntiBioticCreamToMedicationPatch * NumBatchesMedicationPatch)
# @Constraint Constr_2 @Def: The total number of batches of medication patches and anti-biotic creams cannot exceed MaxTotalBatches.
model.addConstr(NumBatchesAntiBioticCream + NumBatchesMedicationPatch <= MaxTotalBatches)
# @Constraint Constr_3 @Def: The total preparation time for medication patches and anti-biotic creams must not exceed AvailableStaffTime minutes.
model.addConstr(PreparationTimeMedicationPatch * NumBatchesMedicationPatch + PreparationTimeAntiBioticCream * NumBatchesAntiBioticCream <= AvailableStaffTime)
# @Constraint Constr_4 @Def: The total materials required for medication patches and anti-biotic creams must not exceed AvailableMaterials units.

DesertTruckCapacity units of goods per trip and takes DesertTruckTime hours per
trip. The company needs to deliver TotalGoodsToDeliver units of goods and
prefers to have more camel caravans than desert trucks. The objective is to

from gurobipy import *

# @Parameter CamelCaravanTime @Def: Time taken for one trip by a camel caravan @Shape: []

# @Parameter DesertTruckCapacity @Def: Amount of goods delivered per trip by a desert truck @Shape: []

nlp4lp_test_263.optimus_code:
# Code automatically generated from OptiMUS

# Problem type: LP        
# Problem description
'''
A biotechnology company has TotalAntibiotics units of antibiotics available,
which are essential for producing first-dose and second-dose vaccines. The
first-dose vaccine requires AntibioticsFirstDose units of antibiotics and
GelatineFirstDose mg of gelatine per unit, while the second-dose vaccine
requires AntibioticsSecondDose units of antibiotics and GelatineSecondDose mg of
gelatine per unit. The production of first-dose vaccines must exceed the
production of second-dose vaccines, and at least MinimumSecondDose second-dose
vaccines must be produced. The objective is to determine the number of first-
dose and second-dose vaccines to manufacture in order to minimize the total
gelatine usage.
'''
# Import necessary libraries
import json
from gurobipy import *
     
# Create a new model
model = Model()

# Load data 
with open("/Users/gaowenzhi/Desktop/optimus-OR-paper/data/new_dataset/sample_datasets/264/parameters.json", "r") as f:
    data = json.load(f)
    
# @Def: definition of a target
# @Shape: shape of a target
        
# Parameters 
# @Parameter TotalAntibiotics @Def: Total number of antibiotics available @Shape: [] 
TotalAntibiotics = data['TotalAntibiotics']
# @Parameter AntibioticsFirstDose @Def: Antibiotics required for one first-dose vaccine @Shape: [] 
AntibioticsFirstDose = data['AntibioticsFirstDose']
# @Parameter GelatineFirstDose @Def: Gelatine required for one first-dose vaccine @Shape: [] 
GelatineFirstDose = data['GelatineFirstDose']
# @Parameter AntibioticsSecondDose @Def: Antibiotics required for one second-dose vaccine @Shape: [] 
AntibioticsSecondDose = data['AntibioticsSecondDose']
# @Parameter GelatineSecondDose @Def: Gelatine required for one second-dose vaccine @Shape: [] 
GelatineSecondDose = data['GelatineSecondDose']
# @Parameter MinimumSecondDose @Def: Minimum number of second-dose vaccines required @Shape: [] 
MinimumSecondDose = data['MinimumSecondDose']

# Variables 
# @Variable FirstDoseVaccines @Def: The number of first-dose vaccines administered @Shape: [] 
FirstDoseVaccines = model.addVar(vtype=GRB.CONTINUOUS, name="FirstDoseVaccines")
# @Variable SecondDoseVaccines @Def: The number of second-dose vaccines administered @Shape: [] 
SecondDoseVaccines = model.addVar(vtype=GRB.CONTINUOUS, name="SecondDoseVaccines")

# Constraints 
# @Constraint Constr_1 @Def: The total antibiotics used for first-dose and second-dose vaccines must not exceed TotalAntibiotics units.
model.addConstr(AntibioticsFirstDose * FirstDoseVaccines + AntibioticsSecondDose * SecondDoseVaccines <= TotalAntibiotics)
# @Constraint Constr_2 @Def: The number of first-dose vaccines produced must be greater than the number of second-dose vaccines produced.
model.addConstr(FirstDoseVaccines >= SecondDoseVaccines)
# @Constraint Constr_3 @Def: At least MinimumSecondDose second-dose vaccines must be produced.
model.addConstr(SecondDoseVaccines >= MinimumSecondDose)

# Objective 
# @Objective Objective @Def: Minimize the total gelatine usage, which is the sum of GelatineFirstDose multiplied by the number of first-dose vaccines and GelatineSecondDose multiplied by the number of second-dose vaccines.
model.setObjective(GelatineFirstDose * FirstDoseVaccines + GelatineSecondDose * SecondDoseVaccines, GRB.MINIMIZE)

# Solve 
model.optimize()

# Extract solution 
solution = {}
variables = {}
objective = []
variables['FirstDoseVaccines'] = FirstDoseVaccines.x
variables['SecondDoseVaccines'] = SecondDoseVaccines.x
solution['variables'] = variables
solution['objective'] = model.objVal
with open('solution.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(solution, f, indent=4)

The number of units of chemical A must not exceed MaxRatioAtoB times the number of units of chemical B.

nlp4lp_test_264.optimus_code:
# Code automatically generated from OptiMUS

# Problem type: LP        
# Problem description
'''
Determine the number of units of chemical A and chemical B to add to the mixer
to minimize the total time for the mixed bread to be ready. Each unit of
chemical A takes TimeChemicalA to become effective, and each unit of chemical B
takes TimeChemicalB to become effective. The number of units of chemical A must
be at least MinChemicalA, the total number of units of chemicals A and B must be
at least MinTotalChemicals, and the number of units of chemical A must not
exceed MaxRatioAtoB times the number of units of chemical B.
'''
# Import necessary libraries
import json
from gurobipy import *
     
# Create a new model
model = Model()

# Load data 
with open("/Users/gaowenzhi/Desktop/optimus-OR-paper/data/new_dataset/sample_datasets/265/parameters.json", "r") as f:
    data = json.load(f)
    
# @Def: definition of a target
# @Shape: shape of a target
        
# Parameters 
# @Parameter TimeChemicalA @Def: Time for one unit of chemical A to become effective @Shape: [] 
TimeChemicalA = data['TimeChemicalA']
# @Parameter TimeChemicalB @Def: Time for one unit of chemical B to become effective @Shape: [] 
TimeChemicalB = data['TimeChemicalB']
# @Parameter MaxRatioAtoB @Def: Maximum ratio of chemical A to chemical B @Shape: [] 
MaxRatioAtoB = data['MaxRatioAtoB']
# @Parameter MinChemicalA @Def: Minimum units of chemical A required @Shape: [] 
MinChemicalA = data['MinChemicalA']
# @Parameter MinTotalChemicals @Def: Minimum total units of chemicals in the mixer @Shape: [] 
MinTotalChemicals = data['MinTotalChemicals']

# Variables 
# @Variable QuantityChemicalA @Def: The number of units of chemical A @Shape: [] 
QuantityChemicalA = model.addVar(vtype=GRB.CONTINUOUS, lb=MinChemicalA, name="QuantityChemicalA")
# @Variable QuantityChemicalB @Def: The number of units of chemical B @Shape: [] 
QuantityChemicalB = model.addVar(vtype=GRB.CONTINUOUS, name="QuantityChemicalB")

# Constraints 
# @Constraint Constr_1 @Def: The number of units of chemical A must be at least MinChemicalA.
model.addConstr(QuantityChemicalA >= MinChemicalA)
# @Constraint Constr_2 @Def: The total number of units of chemicals A and B must be at least MinTotalChemicals.
model.addConstr(QuantityChemicalA + QuantityChemicalB >= MinTotalChemicals)
# @Constraint Constr_3 @Def: The number of units of chemical A must not exceed MaxRatioAtoB times the number of units of chemical B.
model.addConstr(QuantityChemicalA <= MaxRatioAtoB * QuantityChemicalB)

# Objective 
# @Objective Objective @Def: Minimize the total time for the mixed bread to be ready, calculated as (TimeChemicalA * Units of Chemical A) + (TimeChemicalB * Units of Chemical B).
model.setObjective(TimeChemicalA * QuantityChemicalA + TimeChemicalB * QuantityChemicalB, GRB.MINIMIZE)

# Solve 
model.optimize()

# Extract solution 
solution = {}
variables = {}
objective = []
variables['QuantityChemicalA'] = QuantityChemicalA.x
variables['QuantityChemicalB'] = QuantityChemicalB.x
solution['variables'] = variables
solution['objective'] = model.objVal
with open('solution.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(solution, f, indent=4)

nlp4lp_test_267.optimus_code:
# Code automatically generated from OptiMUS

# Problem type: MIP        
# Problem description
'''
A tourism company can purchase sedans and buses. Each sedan seats SedanCapacity
tourists per day and produces SedanPollution units of pollution per day. Each
bus seats BusCapacity tourists per day and produces BusPollution units of
pollution per day. The company is limited to a maximum of MaxPollution units of
pollution per day. To be profitable, the company must serve at least
MinCustomers customers per day. The goal is to determine the number of sedans
and buses to purchase in order to minimize the total number of vehicles needed.
'''
# Import necessary libraries
import json
from gurobipy import *
     
# Create a new model
model = Model()

# Load data 
with open("/Users/gaowenzhi/Desktop/optimus-OR-paper/data/new_dataset/sample_datasets/268/parameters.json", "r") as f:
    data = json.load(f)
    
# @Def: definition of a target
# @Shape: shape of a target
        
# Parameters 
# @Parameter SedanCapacity @Def: Number of tourists that a sedan can seat per day @Shape: [] 
SedanCapacity = data['SedanCapacity']
# @Parameter SedanPollution @Def: Units of pollution resulting from one sedan per day @Shape: [] 
SedanPollution = data['SedanPollution']
# @Parameter BusCapacity @Def: Number of tourists that a bus can seat per day @Shape: [] 
BusCapacity = data['BusCapacity']
# @Parameter BusPollution @Def: Units of pollution resulting from one bus per day @Shape: [] 
BusPollution = data['BusPollution']
# @Parameter MaxPollution @Def: Maximum units of pollution allowed per day @Shape: [] 
MaxPollution = data['MaxPollution']
# @Parameter MinCustomers @Def: Minimum number of customers required per day @Shape: [] 
MinCustomers = data['MinCustomers']

# Variables 
# @Variable NumberOfSedans @Def: The number of sedans used per day @Shape: [] 
NumberOfSedans = model.addVar(vtype=GRB.INTEGER, name="NumberOfSedans")
# @Variable NumberOfBuses @Def: The number of buses used per day @Shape: [] 
NumberOfBuses = model.addVar(vtype=GRB.INTEGER, name="NumberOfBuses")

# Constraints 
# @Constraint Constr_1 @Def: The total pollution produced by the vehicles cannot exceed MaxPollution units per day. This is calculated as SedanPollution multiplied by the number of sedans plus BusPollution multiplied by the number of buses.
model.addConstr(SedanPollution * NumberOfSedans + BusPollution * NumberOfBuses <= MaxPollution)
# @Constraint Constr_2 @Def: The company must serve at least MinCustomers customers per day. This is achieved by ensuring that SedanCapacity multiplied by the number of sedans plus BusCapacity multiplied by the number of buses is at least MinCustomers.
model.addConstr(SedanCapacity * NumberOfSedans + BusCapacity * NumberOfBuses >= MinCustomers)

# Objective 
# @Objective Objective @Def: Minimize the total number of vehicles needed, which is the sum of the number of sedans and the number of buses purchased.
model.setObjective(NumberOfSedans + NumberOfBuses, GRB.MINIMIZE)

# Solve 
model.optimize()

# Extract solution 
solution = {}
variables = {}
objective = []
variables['NumberOfSedans'] = NumberOfSedans.x
variables['NumberOfBuses'] = NumberOfBuses.x
solution['variables'] = variables
solution['objective'] = model.objVal
with open('solution.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(solution, f, indent=4)

nlp4lp_test_268.optimus_code:
# Code automatically generated from OptiMUS

# Problem type: LP        
# Problem description
'''
A dessert shop produces NumDessertTypes different desserts using NumIngredients
different ingredients. The ResourceUsage matrix specifies the amount of each
ingredient required per dessert. The shop must produce more of one dessert type
than another and ensure that at least a proportion MinMatchaProportion of the
total desserts are of a specific type. The total usage of each ingredient must
not exceed AvailableIngredients. The objective is to minimize the total amount
of flavouring required.
'''
# Import necessary libraries
import json
from gurobipy import *
     
# Create a new model
model = Model()

# Load data 
with open("/Users/gaowenzhi/Desktop/optimus-OR-paper/data/new_dataset/sample_datasets/269/parameters.json", "r") as f:
    data = json.load(f)
    
# @Def: definition of a target
# @Shape: shape of a target        
        
# Parameters 
# @Parameter NumDessertTypes @Def: Number of different dessert types @Shape: [] 
NumDessertTypes = data['NumDessertTypes']
# @Parameter NumIngredients @Def: Number of different ingredients @Shape: [] 
NumIngredients = data['NumIngredients']
# @Parameter ResourceUsage @Def: Amount of each ingredient required to produce one unit of each dessert @Shape: ['NumIngredients', 'NumDessertTypes'] 
ResourceUsage = data['ResourceUsage']
# @Parameter AvailableIngredients @Def: Total available units of each ingredient @Shape: ['NumIngredients'] 
AvailableIngredients = data['AvailableIngredients']
# @Parameter MinMatchaProportion @Def: Minimum proportion of desserts that must be matcha ice cream @Shape: [] 
MinMatchaProportion = data['MinMatchaProportion']

# Variables 
# @Variable MatchaIceCream @Def: The number of matcha ice cream desserts produced @Shape: [] 
MatchaIceCream = model.addVar(vtype=GRB.CONTINUOUS, name="MatchaIceCream")
# @Variable OrangeSorbet @Def: The number of orange sorbet desserts produced @Shape: [] 
OrangeSorbet = model.addVar(vtype=GRB.CONTINUOUS, name="OrangeSorbet")

# Constraints 
# @Constraint Constr_1 @Def: The number of matcha ice cream desserts produced must be greater than the number of orange sorbet desserts produced.
model.addConstr(MatchaIceCream >= OrangeSorbet)
# @Constraint Constr_2 @Def: At least a proportion MinMatchaProportion of the total desserts must be matcha ice cream.
model.addConstr(MatchaIceCream >= MinMatchaProportion * (MatchaIceCream + OrangeSorbet))
# @Constraint Constr_3 @Def: The total usage of each ingredient must not exceed AvailableIngredients.
for i in range(NumIngredients):
    model.addConstr(ResourceUsage[i][0] * MatchaIceCream + ResourceUsage[i][1] * OrangeSorbet <= AvailableIngredients[i])

# Objective 
# @Objective Objective @Def: The objective is to minimize the total amount of flavouring required.
model.setObjective(quicksum(ResourceUsage[i][0] * MatchaIceCream + ResourceUsage[i][1] * OrangeSorbet for i in range(NumIngredients)), GRB.MINIMIZE)

# Solve 
model.optimize()

# Extract solution 
solution = {}
variables = {}
objective = []
variables['MatchaIceCream'] = MatchaIceCream.x
variables['OrangeSorbet'] = OrangeSorbet.x
solution['variables'] = variables
solution['objective'] = model.objVal
with open('solution.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(solution, f, indent=4)

problem_info.parametrized_description: The objective is to maximize the number of units of currency N that we end up with at the end of the day, through a sequence of currency transactions. We start with a certain amount Start for each currency, there is a limit Limit to the number of transactions we can perform for each currency, and the exchange rates are given by Rate.

Limit.description: Limit for the number of transactions for currency i

Rate.description: Exchange rate from currency i to currency j

Amount.description: Amount of currency i exchanged to currency j

constraints.description: All exchange amounts are non-negative
Total amount exchanged out of currency i cannot exceed the starting amount plus what is exchanged into currency i
constraints.formulation: \sum_{j=0}^{N-1} Amount_{i,j} \leq Start_i + \sum_{j=0}^{N-1} Rate_{j,i} \times Amount_{j,i}, \quad \forall i \in \{0, \ldots, N-1\}

code.gurobipy:
for i in range(N):
    model.addConstr(quicksum(Amount[i][j] for j in range(N)) <= Start[i] + quicksum(Rate[j][i] * Amount[j][i] for j in range(N)), name=f"FlowBalance_{i}")

constraints.description: Total amount of currency i involved in exchanges does not exceed the limit Limit_i

code.gurobipy:
for i in range(N):
    model.addConstr(quicksum(Amount[i][j] for j in range(N)) + quicksum(Amount[j][i] for j in range(N)) <= Limit[i], name=f"TransLimit_{i}")

objective.description: Maximize the final total number of units of currency N

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(Start[N-1] + quicksum(Rate[j][N-1] * Amount[j][N-1] for j in range(N)) - quicksum(Amount[N-1][j] for j in range(N)), GRB.MAXIMIZE)

problem_info.parametrized_description:
PROBLEM TYPE
LP

INPUT FORMAT 

{
    "A": [[a_i_j for j in 1, ..., N] for i in 1, ..., M],
    "b": [b_i for i in 1, ..., M]
}

OBJECTIVE
We are interested in finding a ball with the largest possible radius, which is entirely contained within the set P (The center of such a ball is called the Chebychev center of P).

M.description: The number of inequalities defining the set P

N.description: The ambient space dimension of the set P

A.description: The coefficients of the linear inequalities defining the set P

B.description: The right-hand side of the inequalities defining the set P

Center.description: Coordinates of the center of the ball (Chebyshev center)

Radius.description: Radius of the largest inscribed ball

constraints.description: The radius of the ball is non-negative
The ball must be completely within the set P, which is defined by the constraints Ax <= b. For each inequality i, the constraint is a_i^T y + ||a_i|| * r <= b_i.
constraints.formulation: \sum_{j=0}^{N-1} A_{i,j} \times Center_j + \|a_i\|_2 \times Radius \leq B_i, \quad \forall i \in \{0, \ldots, M-1\}

code.gurobipy:
import math
for i in range(M):
    norm_ai = math.sqrt(sum(A[i][j] ** 2 for j in range(N)))
    model.addConstr(quicksum(A[i][j] * Center[j] for j in range(N)) + norm_ai * Radius <= B[i], name=f"BallInP_{i}")

objective.description: Maximize the radius r of the ball centered at the Chebyshev center that lies entirely within P

problem_info.parametrized_description: We are aiming at maximizing total net income subject to the Cash availability and machine capacity limitations. The problem parameters include: the initial Cash available, the Hour(s) required to produce each of the P products, the Cost to produce each of the products, the Price at which each product can be sold, the InvestPercentage of income from each product to be reinvested, the UpgradeHours required for upgrading the machinery, the UpgradeCost associated with upgrading, and the total available AvailableHours for machine operation.

Hour.description: Hours required to produce one unit of product i

Cost.description: Cost to produce one unit of product i

Price.description: Selling price for one unit of product i

InvestPercentage.description: Percentage of income to reinvest for product i

UpgradeHours.description: Hours required for upgrading the machinery

UpgradeCost.description: Cost associated with upgrading the machinery

Production.description: Number of units of product i to produce

Upgrade.description: Binary variable indicating whether to upgrade machinery (1) or not (0)

constraints.description: Production quantities are non-negative

code.gurobipy:
for i in range(P):
    model.addConstr(Production[i] >= 0, name=f"ProdNonNeg_{i}")

constraints.description: Upgrade variable is binary (0 or 1)
Total production hours for all products do not exceed available hours (plus upgrade hours if upgrading)

code.gurobipy: model.addConstr(quicksum(Hour[i] * Production[i] for i in range(P)) <= AvailableHours + UpgradeHours * Upgrade, name="MachineCapacity")

constraints.description: Total production cost plus upgrade cost must not exceed the available cash plus reinvested revenue
constraints.formulation: \sum_{i=0}^{P-1} Cost_i \times Production_i + UpgradeCost \times Upgrade \leq Cash + \sum_{i=0}^{P-1} InvestPercentage_i \times Price_i \times Production_i

code.gurobipy: model.addConstr(quicksum(Cost[i] * Production[i] for i in range(P)) + UpgradeCost * Upgrade <= Cash + quicksum(InvestPercentage[i] * Price[i] * Production[i] for i in range(P)), name="CashBalance")

objective.description: Maximize total net income considering production, selling price, cost, and reinvestment
objective.formulation: \max \ \sum_{i=0}^{P-1} (Price_i - Cost_i - InvestPercentage_i \times Price_i) \times Production_i - UpgradeCost \times Upgrade

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(quicksum((Price[i] - Cost[i] - InvestPercentage[i] * Price[i]) * Production[i] for i in range(P)) - UpgradeCost * Upgrade, GRB.MAXIMIZE)

problem_info.parametrized_description: We want to maximize the daily profit of the company, which is the total revenue minus the total cost. The company has N different products, each requiring AssemblyHour hours for assembly, TestingHour hours for testing, and with a material cost of MaterialCost. The company cannot exceed MaxAssembly hours for assembly and MaxTesting hours for testing. Each product can be sold at Price, but there is an opportunity to work MaxOvertimeAssembly overtime hours for assembly at a cost of OvertimeAssemblyCost per hour. Additionally, a material discount of MaterialDiscount is offered when a purchase reaches the DiscountThreshold.

AssemblyHour.description: Hours required to assemble product i

TestingHour.description: Hours required to test product i

MaxAssembly.description: Maximum total hours available for assembly

MaxTesting.description: Maximum total hours available for testing

MaxOvertimeAssembly.description: Maximum overtime hours available for assembly

OvertimeAssemblyCost.description: Overtime cost for assembly per hour

MaterialDiscount.description: Discount on material cost when a certain threshold is reached

DiscountThreshold.description: Threshold for the quantity to reach to obtain the material discount

UnitsProduced.description: Number of units of product i produced

OvertimeAssembly.description: Number of overtime assembly hours scheduled

MaterialBought.description: Total amount spent on raw materials

DiscountApplied.description: Binary variable indicating whether the material discount is applied (1) or not (0)

constraints.description: Total assembly hours must not exceed MaxAssembly hours plus overtime assembly hours

code.gurobipy: model.addConstr(gurobipy.quicksum(AssemblyHour[i] * UnitsProduced[i] for i in range(N)) <= MaxAssembly + OvertimeAssembly, name="AssemblyCapacity")

constraints.description: Total testing hours must not exceed MaxTesting hours

code.gurobipy: model.addConstr(gurobipy.quicksum(TestingHour[i] * UnitsProduced[i] for i in range(N)) <= MaxTesting, name="TestingCapacity")

constraints.description: The number of products produced is non-negative

code.gurobipy:
for i in range(N):
    model.addConstr(UnitsProduced[i] >= 0, name=f"NonNegProd_{i}")

constraints.description: The number of overtime assembly hours used is at most MaxOvertimeAssembly
MaterialBought equals the total material cost of all products produced

code.gurobipy: model.addConstr(MaterialBought == gurobipy.quicksum(MaterialCost[i] * UnitsProduced[i] for i in range(N)), name="MaterialBoughtDef")

constraints.description: If DiscountApplied is 1 then MaterialBought must be at least DiscountThreshold; if 0 then MaterialBought can be below threshold
If MaterialBought exceeds DiscountThreshold then discount must be applied

code.gurobipy:
BigM = 1e7
model.addConstr(MaterialBought <= DiscountThreshold + (BigM - DiscountThreshold) * DiscountApplied, name="DiscountForce")

objective.description: Maximize the total daily profit, which is the total revenue minus the total cost including overtime costs, regular material costs, and considering material discounts
objective.formulation: \max \ \sum_{i=1}^{N} Price_{i} \times UnitsProduced_{i} - OvertimeAssemblyCost \times OvertimeAssembly - MaterialBought \times (1 - \frac{MaterialDiscount}{100} \times DiscountApplied)

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(gurobipy.quicksum(Price[i] * UnitsProduced[i] for i in range(N)) - OvertimeAssemblyCost * OvertimeAssembly - MaterialBought * (1 - (MaterialDiscount / 100) * DiscountApplied), GRB.MAXIMIZE)

problem_info.parametrized_description: We want to know how many times should we execute each process to maximize the revenue for the next month (you can assume that the number of times that each process is executed is a floating point number). The problem involves O options, P price points, and L processes with Allocated resources, Price at each price point, Input required for each option in each process, Output produced for each price point in each process, and Cost for each process.

Input.description: Input resources required for each option in each process

Output.description: Output produced for each price point in each process

Execute.description: Number of times process l is executed

constraints.description: Each process execution number is non-negative

code.gurobipy:
for l in range(L):
    model.addConstr(Execute[l] >= 0, name=f"NonNegExec_{l}")

constraints.description: Total input usage for each crude type across all processes does not exceed allocated resources

code.gurobipy:
for i in range(O):
    model.addConstr(gurobipy.quicksum(Input[l][i] * Execute[l] for l in range(L)) <= Allocated[i], name=f"ResourceLimit_{i}")

objective.description: Maximize the total profit, which is the revenue from selling products minus the cost of running processes

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(gurobipy.quicksum((gurobipy.quicksum(Price[p] * Output[l][p] for p in range(P)) - Cost[l]) * Execute[l] for l in range(L)), GRB.MAXIMIZE)

problem_info.parametrized_description: We want to know how many shares the investor needs to sell in order to raise the needed money, net of capital gains and transaction costs, K, while maximizing the expected value of his portfolio next year. The investor initially bought Bought shares at a purchase price of BuyPrice per share. The current market price and expected future market price of the shares are CurrentPrice and FuturePrice, respectively. The transaction cost rate per share sold is TransactionRate, and the capital gains tax rate is TaxRate.

Bought.description: Amount of each share initially bought

CurrentPrice.description: Current market price of each share

FuturePrice.description: Expected future market price of each share

TransactionRate.description: Transaction cost rate per share sold

TaxRate.description: Capital gains tax rate on the profit from selling shares

K.description: Amount of money the investor needs to raise

Sell.description: Number of shares of stock i to sell

constraints.description: Number of shares sold is non-negative

code.gurobipy:
for i in range(N):
    model.addConstr(Sell[i] >= 0, name=f"NonNegSell_{i}")

constraints.description: Number of shares sold is less than or equal to Bought

code.gurobipy:
for i in range(N):
    model.addConstr(Sell[i] <= Bought[i], name=f"MaxSell_{i}")

constraints.description: Amount raised from selling shares after deducting transaction costs and capital gains taxes is at least K
constraints.formulation: \sum_{i=1}^{N} \left( CurrentPrice_{i} \times Sell_{i} - \frac{TransactionRate}{100} \times CurrentPrice_{i} \times Sell_{i} - \frac{TaxRate}{100} \times \max(CurrentPrice_{i} - BuyPrice_{i}, 0) \times Sell_{i} \right) \geq K

code.gurobipy: model.addConstr(gurobipy.quicksum(CurrentPrice[i] * Sell[i] - (TransactionRate / 100) * CurrentPrice[i] * Sell[i] - (TaxRate / 100) * max(CurrentPrice[i] - BuyPrice[i], 0) * Sell[i] for i in range(N)) >= K, name="RaiseMoney")

objective.description: Maximize the expected value of the investor's portfolio next year

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(gurobipy.quicksum(FuturePrice[i] * (Bought[i] - Sell[i]) for i in range(N)), GRB.MAXIMIZE)

problem_info.parametrized_description: A company is dealing with I different items over a planning horizon of M months. The buy price of item i in month m is given by BuyPrice. All items can be sold after refining for the same SellPrice. Each item is either a vegetable or not, which is indicated by IsVegetable. There is a limited refining capability where at most MaxVegetableRefiningPerMonth of vegetables and MaxNonVegetableRefiningPerMonth of non-vegetables can be refined in any given month. The company has a storage size of StorageSize and incurs a StorageCost for storing items per month. The refined products must fall within a hardness range of MinHardness to MaxHardness, where the hardness of item i is Hardness. Initially, the storage contains an amount of InitialAmount of items. What buying, storing, and manufacturing policy should the company pursue in order to maximize profit?

BuyPrice.description: Month m buy price of item i

SellPrice.description: Sell price of the products after refining

IsVegetable.description: Indicator whether item i is a vegetable

MaxVegetableRefiningPerMonth.description: Maximum refining capability for vegetables per month

MaxNonVegetableRefiningPerMonth.description: Maximum refining capability for non-vegetables per month

StorageCost.description: Cost for storing items per month

MaxHardness.description: The maximum hardness allowed for the products after refining

MinHardness.description: The minimum hardness required for the products after refining

InitialAmount.description: Initial amount of items in storage at the beginning of the planning horizon

Buy.description: Amount of item i bought in month m

Refine.description: Amount of item i refined in month m

Storage.description: Amount of item i stored at the end of month m

constraints.description: All items bought, stored, and refined are non-negative
Hardness of refined items must be at least MinHardness (linearized)

code.gurobipy:
for m in range(M):
    model.addConstr(gurobipy.quicksum(Hardness[i] * Refine[m][i] for i in range(I)) >= MinHardness * gurobipy.quicksum(Refine[m][i] for i in range(I)), name=f"MinHardness_{m}")

constraints.description: Hardness of refined items must not exceed MaxHardness (linearized)

code.gurobipy:
for m in range(M):
    model.addConstr(gurobipy.quicksum(Hardness[i] * Refine[m][i] for i in range(I)) <= MaxHardness * gurobipy.quicksum(Refine[m][i] for i in range(I)), name=f"MaxHardness_{m}")

constraints.description: At most MaxVegetableRefiningPerMonth vegetables can be refined per month

code.gurobipy:
for m in range(M):
    model.addConstr(gurobipy.quicksum(Refine[m][i] for i in range(I) if IsVegetable[i]) <= MaxVegetableRefiningPerMonth, name=f"MaxVegRefine_{m}")

constraints.description: At most MaxNonVegetableRefiningPerMonth non-vegetables can be refined per month

code.gurobipy:
for m in range(M):
    model.addConstr(gurobipy.quicksum(Refine[m][i] for i in range(I) if not IsVegetable[i]) <= MaxNonVegetableRefiningPerMonth, name=f"MaxNonVegRefine_{m}")

constraints.description: Total storage used per item must not exceed StorageSize
Inventory balance: storage at end of month equals previous storage plus purchases minus refining. For the first month, previous storage is InitialAmount.
constraints.formulation: Storage_{m,i} = Storage_{m-1,i} + Buy_{m,i} - Refine_{m,i}, \quad \forall m, i \quad (Storage_{0,i} = InitialAmount)

constraints.description: At the end of the last month, storage must equal InitialAmount for each item

code.gurobipy:
for i in range(I):
    model.addConstr(Storage[M-1][i] == InitialAmount, name=f"FinalStorage_{i}")

objective.description: Maximize the company's profit by determining the optimal buying, storing, and manufacturing policy
objective.formulation: \max \ \sum_{m=1}^{M} \left( SellPrice \times \sum_{i=1}^{I} Refine_{m,i} - \sum_{i=1}^{I} BuyPrice_{m,i} \times Buy_{m,i} - StorageCost \times \sum_{i=1}^{I} Storage_{m,i} \right)

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(gurobipy.quicksum(SellPrice * gurobipy.quicksum(Refine[m][i] for i in range(I)) - gurobipy.quicksum(BuyPrice[m][i] * Buy[m][i] for i in range(I)) - StorageCost * gurobipy.quicksum(Storage[m][i] for i in range(I)) for m in range(M)), GRB.MAXIMIZE)

problem_info.parametrized_description:
LP or MILP

What buying, storing and manufacturing policy should the company pursue in order to maximize profit?

{
    "buy_price": [[BuyPrice for i in 1,...,I] for m in 1,...,M],
    "sell_price": SellPrice,
    "is_vegetable": [IsVegetable for i in 1,...,I],
    "max_vegetable_refining_per_month": MaxVegRefiningPerMonth,
    "max_non_vegetable_refining_per_month": MaxNonVegRefiningPerMonth,
    "storage_size": StorageSize,
    "storage_cost": StorageCost,
    "max_hardness": MaxHardness,
    "min_hardness": MinHardness,
    "hardness": [Hardness for i in 1,...,I],
    "init_amount": InitialAmount,
    "min_usage": MinUsage,
    "dependencies": [Dependencies for i in 1,...,I]
}

BuyPrice.description: Buying price of good i in month m

IsVegetable.description: Indicator whether good i is a vegetable or not

MaxVegRefiningPerMonth.description: Maximum refining capacity for vegetables per month

MaxNonVegRefiningPerMonth.description: Maximum refining capacity for non-vegetables per month

StorageCost.description: Storage cost per unit per month

MaxHardness.description: Maximum hardness of goods that can be processed

MinHardness.description: Minimum hardness of goods that can be processed

InitialAmount.description: Initial amount of goods available for processing

Buy.description: Amount of oil i bought in month m

Refine.description: Amount of oil i refined in month m

Storage.description: Amount of oil i stored at the end of month m

Use.description: Binary variable indicating whether oil i is used in month m

constraints.description: Maximum amount of vegetables that can be refined per month is defined by MaxVegRefiningPerMonth

code.gurobipy:
for m in range(M):
    model.addConstr(gurobipy.quicksum(Refine[m][i] for i in range(I) if IsVegetable[i]) <= MaxVegRefiningPerMonth, name=f"MaxVegRefine_{m}")

constraints.description: Maximum amount of non-vegetables that can be refined per month is defined by MaxNonVegRefiningPerMonth

code.gurobipy:
for m in range(M):
    model.addConstr(gurobipy.quicksum(Refine[m][i] for i in range(I) if not IsVegetable[i]) <= MaxNonVegRefiningPerMonth, name=f"MaxNonVegRefine_{m}")

constraints.description: The storage has a size limit defined by StorageSize for each oil
Hardness of blended product must be within MinHardness and MaxHardness range (linearized)
constraints.formulation: MinHardness \times \sum_{i=1}^{I} Refine_{m,i} \leq \sum_{i=1}^{I} Hardness_{i} \times Refine_{m,i} \leq MaxHardness \times \sum_{i=1}^{I} Refine_{m,i}, \quad \forall m

constraints.description: If oil i is used in month m, at least MinUsage tons must be refined; linking binary Use variable to Refine
constraints.formulation: MinUsage \times Use_{m,i} \leq Refine_{m,i} \leq StorageSize \times Use_{m,i}, \quad \forall m, i

constraints.description: At most 3 oils can be used in any month

code.gurobipy:
for m in range(M):
    model.addConstr(gurobipy.quicksum(Use[m][i] for i in range(I)) <= 3, name=f"MaxOils_{m}")

constraints.description: Dependencies between oils: if oil i is used and Dependencies[i][j] is 1, then oil j must also be used
Inventory balance: storage at end of month equals previous storage plus purchases minus refining. Initial storage is InitialAmount.
constraints.formulation: Storage_{m,i} = Storage_{m-1,i} + Buy_{m,i} - Refine_{m,i}, \quad \forall m, i \quad (Storage_{0,i} = InitialAmount)

constraints.description: At the end of the last month, storage must equal InitialAmount for each oil

code.gurobipy:
for i in range(I):
    model.addConstr(Storage[M-1][i] == InitialAmount, name=f"FinalStorage_{i}")

constraints.description: All variables are non-negative and Use is binary
constraints.formulation: Buy_{m,i} \geq 0, \quad Refine_{m,i} \geq 0, \quad Storage_{m,i} \geq 0, \quad Use_{m,i} \in \{0,1\}, \quad \forall m, i

objective.description: Maximize the company's profit from its buying, storing, and manufacturing policy
objective.formulation: \max \ \sum_{m=1}^{M} \left( SellPrice \times \sum_{i=1}^{I} Refine_{m,i} - \sum_{i=1}^{I} BuyPrice_{i,m} \times Buy_{m,i} - StorageCost \times \sum_{i=1}^{I} Storage_{m,i} \right)

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(gurobipy.quicksum(SellPrice * gurobipy.quicksum(Refine[m][i] for i in range(I)) - gurobipy.quicksum(BuyPrice[i][m] * Buy[m][i] for i in range(I)) - StorageCost * gurobipy.quicksum(Storage[m][i] for i in range(I)) for m in range(M)), GRB.MAXIMIZE)

problem_info.parametrized_description: What selling, storing and manufacturing policy should the company pursue in order to maximize profit? The company has M machines and produces K different products. Each product k has a profit of Profit. The time taken to manufacture product k on machine m is given by Time. There are maintenance times for each machine m in each time period i, denoted by Maintain. The limit for storing product k for time period i is given by Limit, and the company has a store price of StorePrice. The company aims to keep a quantity of KeepQuantity for product k, and there are WorkHours working hours available for production.

Time.description: Time taken by product k on machine m

Maintain.description: Maintenance time for machine m in time period i

Limit.description: Limit of storing product k for time period i

KeepQuantity.description: Quantity of product k to keep

Sell.description: Amount of product k sold in period i

Manufacture.description: Amount of product k manufactured in period i

Store.description: Amount of product k stored at end of period i

constraints.description: Machine capacity constraint: total production time on each machine type in each period must not exceed available hours (considering maintenance)
constraints.formulation: \sum_{k=1}^{NumProducts} Time_{k,m} \times Manufacture_{k,i} \leq NumWorkHours \times 24 \times (NumMachines_{m} - Maintain_{m,i}), \quad \forall m, i

constraints.description: Sales limit: amount sold of product k in period i must not exceed the marketing limit
Storage limit: at most 100 units of each product can be stored at any time
Inventory balance: storage at end of period equals previous storage plus manufacturing minus sales. No initial stock.
constraints.formulation: Store_{k,i} = Store_{k,i-1} + Manufacture_{k,i} - Sell_{k,i}, \quad \forall k, i \quad (Store_{k,0} = Manufacture_{k,0} - Sell_{k,0})

constraints.description: At the end of the last period, we must have KeepQuantity of each product in storage

code.gurobipy:
for k in range(NumProducts):
    model.addConstr(Store[k][NumPeriods-1] == KeepQuantity, name=f"FinalStock_{k}")

constraints.description: Products must have non-negative production, sales, and storage quantities

objective.description: Maximize the company's profit from selling its products while accounting for storage costs

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(gurobipy.quicksum(Profit[k] * Sell[k][i] - StorePrice * Store[k][i] for k in range(NumProducts) for i in range(NumPeriods)), GRB.MAXIMIZE)

problem_info.parametrized_description: What maintaining, selling, storing, and manufacturing policy should the company pursue in order to maximize profit? The company has M machines, each with a specific downtime Downtime. There are K products, each with an associated profit Profit and a production time on each machine given by Time. Products must adhere to limits given by Limit. The price for storing products is StorePrice, and they must keep a quantity of KeepQuantity in the store. The company has WorkHours work hours available for all machines combined.

Time.description: Time required to produce each product on each machine

Limit.description: Limit for each product on constraints

KeepQuantity.description: Quantity of products to keep in store

NumMachines.description: Number of each type of machine

Sell.description: Amount of product p sold in month t

Manufacture.description: Amount of product p manufactured in month t

Store.description: Amount of product p stored at end of month t

Maintain.description: Number of machines of type m under maintenance in month t

constraints.description: Machine capacity constraint: total production time on each machine type in each month must not exceed available hours (two shifts, considering maintenance)
constraints.formulation: \sum_{p=1}^{P} Time_{p,m} \times Manufacture_{p,t} \leq WorkHours \times 2 \times 24 \times (NumMachines_{m} - Maintain_{m,t}), \quad \forall m, t

constraints.description: Sales limit: amount sold of product p in month t must not exceed the marketing limit
Storage limit: at most 100 units of each product can be stored at any time
Inventory balance: storage at end of month equals previous storage plus manufacturing minus sales. No initial stock.
constraints.formulation: Store_{p,t} = Store_{p,t-1} + Manufacture_{p,t} - Sell_{p,t}, \quad \forall p, t \quad (Store_{p,0} = Manufacture_{p,0} - Sell_{p,0})

constraints.description: At the end of the last month, we must have KeepQuantity of each product in storage

code.gurobipy:
for p in range(P):
    model.addConstr(Store[p][T-1] == KeepQuantity, name=f"FinalStock_{p}")

constraints.description: Each machine type must have exactly Downtime months of maintenance scheduled over the planning horizon

code.gurobipy:
for m in range(M):
    model.addConstr(gurobipy.quicksum(Maintain[m][t] for t in range(T)) == Downtime[m] * NumMachines[m], name=f"TotalMaintenance_{m}")

constraints.description: Number of machines under maintenance cannot exceed the number of machines of that type
All production, sales, and storage variables must be non-negative

objective.description: Maximize the company's profit from its manufacturing and sales operations while considering maintenance, storage and work hour constraints

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(gurobipy.quicksum(Profit[p] * Sell[p][t] - StorePrice * Store[p][t] for p in range(P) for t in range(T)), GRB.MAXIMIZE)

problem_info.parametrized_description: The company’s declared objective is to minimize redundancy. Requirement are given as K different resources each having I specific requirements, where Strength is the strength of each type of resource, LessOneWaste is the value when wastage of resources is less than optimal, MoreOneWaste is the value when wastage of resources is more than optimal. Recruit is the number of recruited resources of each type, CostRedundancy is the cost of redundancy for each type of resource, NumOverman signifies the number of overmanned positions, CostOverman is the cost of overmanning for each type of resource, and NumShortwork represents the number of shortworked positions with CostShort as the cost of short working for each type of resource. How should they operate in order to do this?

K.description: Total number of different types of resources

I.description: Number of different requirements for each type of resource

Strength.description: Strength of each type of resource

LessOneWaste.description: Value when wastage of resources is less than optimal

MoreOneWaste.description: Value when wastage of resources is more than optimal

Recruit.description: Number of recruited resources of each type

CostRedundancy.description: Cost of redundancy for each type of resource

CostOverman.description: Cost of overmanning for each type of resource

CostShort.description: Cost of short working for each type of resource

Requirement.description: Manpower requirements for each type of resource over the years

RecruitNum.description: Number of workers recruited in category k in year i

Overmanning.description: Number of overmanned workers in category k in year i

Short.description: Number of short-time workers in category k in year i

Redundancy.description: Number of workers made redundant in category k in year i

constraints.description: All decision variables must be non-negative
constraints.formulation: RecruitNum_{k,i} \geq 0, \quad Overmanning_{k,i} \geq 0, \quad Short_{k,i} \geq 0, \quad Redundancy_{k,i} \geq 0, \quad \forall k, i

constraints.description: Recruitment in each category is limited to the maximum recruit number
Short-time working in each category is limited to NumShortwork
Total overmanning across all categories is limited to NumOverman in each year

code.gurobipy:
for i in range(I):
    model.addConstr(gurobipy.quicksum(Overmanning[k][i] for k in range(K)) <= NumOverman, name=f"MaxOverman_{i}")

constraints.description: Manpower balance: workforce in each category and year equals the requirement plus overmanning minus short-time contribution. First year uses Strength as the starting workforce.
constraints.formulation: Requirement_{k,i} + Overmanning_{k,i} - 0.5 \times Short_{k,i} = (1 - MoreOneWaste_{k}) \times (PreviousWorkforce_{k,i}) + (1 - LessOneWaste_{k}) \times RecruitNum_{k,i} - Redundancy_{k,i}, \quad \forall k, i

objective.description: Minimize the total number of redundancies across all categories and years

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(gurobipy.quicksum(Redundancy[k][i] for k in range(K) for i in range(I)), GRB.MINIMIZE)

problem_info.parametrized_description: The company’s declared objective is to minimize costs. It has K categories of requirements, with each category consisting of I different requirements. The strength of each requirement category is denoted by Strength, whereas the cost reductions per unit less waste in each category and the additional costs per unit more waste in each category are represented by LessOneWaste and MoreOneWaste, respectively. The number of recruits in each category is given by Recruit, and the cost of redundancy per recruit in each category is CostRedundancy. The company can allow NumOverman over-managed categories, with each over-managed category incurring a cost of CostOverman. Similarly, NumShortwork short-work categories are permissible, each one resulting in a cost of CostShort. How should they operate in order to minimize these costs?

LessOneWaste.description: Cost reduction per unit less waste in each category

MoreOneWaste.description: Additional cost per unit more waste in each category

Recruit.description: Number of recruits in each category

CostRedundancy.description: Cost of redundancy per recruit in each category

CostOverman.description: Cost of over-managing in each category

CostShort.description: Cost of short-work in each category

Requirement.description: Estimated manpower requirements for manpower k in year i

RecruitNum.description: Number of workers recruited in category k in year i

Overmanning.description: Number of overmanned workers in category k in year i

Short.description: Number of short-time workers in category k in year i

Redundancy.description: Number of workers made redundant in category k in year i

constraints.description: All decision variables must be non-negative
constraints.formulation: RecruitNum_{k,i} \geq 0, \quad Overmanning_{k,i} \geq 0, \quad Short_{k,i} \geq 0, \quad Redundancy_{k,i} \geq 0, \quad \forall k, i

constraints.description: Recruitment in each category is limited to the maximum recruit number
Short-time working in each category is limited to NumShortwork
Total overmanning across all categories is limited to NumOverman in each year

code.gurobipy:
for i in range(I):
    model.addConstr(gurobipy.quicksum(Overmanning[k][i] for k in range(K)) <= NumOverman, name=f"MaxOverman_{i}")

constraints.description: Manpower balance: workforce in each category and year equals the requirement plus overmanning minus short-time contribution. First year uses Strength as the starting workforce.
constraints.formulation: Requirement_{k,i} + Overmanning_{k,i} - 0.5 \times Short_{k,i} = (1 - MoreOneWaste_{k}) \times PreviousWorkforce_{k,i} + (1 - LessOneWaste_{k}) \times RecruitNum_{k,i} - Redundancy_{k,i}, \quad \forall k, i

objective.description: Minimize the overall costs of operations including redundancy, overmanning, and short working
objective.formulation: \min \ \sum_{k=1}^{K} \sum_{i=1}^{I} \left( CostRedundancy_{k} \times Redundancy_{k,i} + CostOverman_{k} \times Overmanning_{k,i} + CostShort_{k} \times Short_{k,i} \right)

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(gurobipy.quicksum(CostRedundancy[k] * Redundancy[k][i] + CostOverman[k] * Overmanning[k][i] + CostShort[k] * Short[k][i] for k in range(K) for i in range(I)), GRB.MINIMIZE)

problem_info.parametrized_description: To maximize profit, which mines should be operated each year and how much should they produce? The company has K mines and can operate at most MaxWork in any given year. For each mine k, there is a royalty cost of Royalty, a production limit of Limit, and the material extracted has a quality of Quality. Each client i requires a material with at least RequiredQuality. The price per unit of material sold is Price, with a discount Discount applied to the royalty cost for each unit sold.

MaxWork.description: Maximum number of mines that can be operated in a year

NumYears.description: Number of years the company will be operating

Royalty.description: Royalty cost for operating mine k

Quality.description: Quality of the material from mine k

RequiredQuality.description: Required quality of the material for each year

Price.description: Price per unit of material sold

Discount.description: Discount rate on the royalty cost per unit of material sold

IsOperated.description: Binary variable indicating whether mine k is operated in year i

Amount.description: Amount of ore produced by mine k in year i

constraints.description: Number of operated mines does not exceed MaxWork in each year

code.gurobipy:
for i in range(NumYears):
    model.addConstr(gurobipy.quicksum(IsOperated[k][i] for k in range(K)) <= MaxWork, name=f"MaxMines_{i}")

constraints.description: Each mine k produces a non-negative amount of material
Each mine k has a production limit of Limit, and can only produce if operated
The blended quality of ore in each year must equal the required quality (weighted average)

code.gurobipy:
for i in range(NumYears):
    model.addConstr(gurobipy.quicksum(Quality[k] * Amount[k][i] for k in range(K)) == RequiredQuality[i] * gurobipy.quicksum(Amount[k][i] for k in range(K)), name=f"QualityReq_{i}")

constraints.description: If a mine is operated in a future year, it must be kept open (operated or maintained) in all previous years
constraints.formulation: IsOperated_{k,i} \leq IsOperated_{k,i-1}, \quad \forall k, \forall i \geq 2 \quad \text{(once closed, stays closed)}

objective.description: Maximize the company's discounted profit from operating the mines and selling the material produced

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(gurobipy.quicksum((1 / (1 + Discount) ** (i + 1)) * (Price * gurobipy.quicksum(Amount[k][i] for k in range(K)) - gurobipy.quicksum(Royalty[k] * IsOperated[k][i] for k in range(K))) for i in range(NumYears)), GRB.MAXIMIZE)

problem_info.parametrized_description: How should the farmer operate over the next five years to maximize profit using AllSpace, NHeifers, NMilk, HeiferSpace, CowSpace, CowCalve, PriceBullock, PriceHeifer, PriceCow, LossHeifer, LossCow, RevenueMilk, NMaxCow, CowOutlay, CowGrain, CowSugar, SugarPerAcre, SpaceGrain, GrainDivision, GrainYield, BuyPriceGrain, SellPriceGrain, BuyPriceSugar, SellPriceSugar, LabourHeifers, LabourCow, LabourGrain, LabourSugar, ExtraHeifers, ExtraCow, ExtraGrain, ExtraSugar, LabourCost, LabourHour, and ExtraLabourCost?

AllSpace.description: Total space available for all animals and activities

NMaxCow.description: Maximum number of cows that can be housed

CowGrain.description: Amount of grain consumed by a cow

CowSugar.description: Amount of sugar consumed by a cow

SugarPerAcre.description: Amount of sugar produced per acre

SpaceGrain.description: Space required per acre of grain

GrainDivision.description: Division of overall space dedicated to grain production

LabourGrain.description: Labour hours required for grain production

LabourSugar.description: Labour hours required for sugar production

SellHeifer.description: Number of heifers sold in year t

SpaceGrainUsed.description: Acres used for grain production in year t

SpaceSugar.description: Acres used for sugar beet production in year t

NumHeifers.description: Number of heifers at the start of year t

NumCows.description: Number of dairy cows in year t

BuyGrain.description: Amount of grain bought in year t

BuySugar.description: Amount of sugar beet bought in year t

SellGrain.description: Amount of grain sold in year t

SellSugar.description: Amount of sugar beet sold in year t

ExtraLabour.description: Extra labour hours needed in year t

NumExtraCow.description: Number of cows beyond NMaxCow requiring capital outlay in year t

constraints.description: Total space used by heifers, cows, grain, and sugar beet cannot exceed AllSpace each year
constraints.formulation: HeiferSpace \times NumHeifers_{t} + CowSpace \times NumCows_{t} + SpaceGrainUsed_{t} + SpaceSugar_{t} \leq AllSpace, \quad \forall t

code.gurobipy:
for t in range(5):
    model.addConstr(HeiferSpace * NumHeifers[t] + CowSpace * NumCows[t] + SpaceGrainUsed[t] + SpaceSugar[t] <= AllSpace, name=f"TotalSpace_{t}")

constraints.description: Number of heifers and cows are non-negative
Heifer balance: new heifers come from calving (half of calves are heifers) minus sold heifers minus losses. At year 0, initial heifers are NHeifers.
constraints.formulation: NumHeifers_{t} = (1 - LossHeifer) \times (PreviousHeifers_{t} - SellHeifer_{t}) + 0.5 \times CowCalve \times NumCows_{t}, \quad \forall t

constraints.description: Cow balance: new cows come from heifers that matured (2 years old), minus losses and sales of old cows
Number of acres allocated to grain production cannot exceed SpaceGrain each year

code.gurobipy:
for t in range(5):
    model.addConstr(SpaceGrainUsed[t] <= SpaceGrain, name=f"GrainSpaceLimit_{t}")

constraints.description: Grain balance: grain produced plus bought must cover grain consumed by cows plus grain sold
constraints.formulation: \sum_{g=1}^{NumGrainDivisions} GrainYield_{g} \times GrainDivision_{g} \times \frac{SpaceGrainUsed_{t}}{SpaceGrain} + BuyGrain_{t} \geq CowGrain \times NumCows_{t} + SellGrain_{t}, \quad \forall t

constraints.description: Sugar beet balance: sugar produced plus bought must cover sugar consumed by cows plus sugar sold
constraints.formulation: SugarPerAcre \times SpaceSugar_{t} + BuySugar_{t} \geq CowSugar \times NumCows_{t} + SellSugar_{t}, \quad \forall t

code.gurobipy:
for t in range(5):
    model.addConstr(SugarPerAcre * SpaceSugar[t] + BuySugar[t] >= CowSugar * NumCows[t] + SellSugar[t], name=f"SugarBalance_{t}")

constraints.description: Labour balance: total labour used minus base labour gives extra labour needed
constraints.formulation: LabourHeifers \times NumHeifers_{t} + LabourCow \times NumCows_{t} + LabourGrain \times SpaceGrainUsed_{t} + LabourSugar \times SpaceSugar_{t} \leq LabourHour + ExtraLabour_{t}, \quad \forall t

code.gurobipy:
for t in range(5):
    model.addConstr(LabourHeifers * NumHeifers[t] + LabourCow * NumCows[t] + LabourGrain * SpaceGrainUsed[t] + LabourSugar * SpaceSugar[t] <= LabourHour + ExtraLabour[t], name=f"LabourBalance_{t}")

constraints.description: Number of extra cows beyond NMaxCow
All sell, buy, space, and extra labour variables must be non-negative
constraints.formulation: SellHeifer_{t} \geq 0, \quad SpaceGrainUsed_{t} \geq 0, \quad SpaceSugar_{t} \geq 0, \quad BuyGrain_{t} \geq 0, \quad BuySugar_{t} \geq 0, \quad SellGrain_{t} \geq 0, \quad SellSugar_{t} \geq 0, \quad ExtraLabour_{t} \geq 0, \quad \forall t

code.gurobipy:
for t in range(5):
    model.addConstr(SellHeifer[t] >= 0, name=f"NonNegSellHeifer_{t}")
    model.addConstr(SpaceGrainUsed[t] >= 0, name=f"NonNegSpaceGrain_{t}")
    model.addConstr(SpaceSugar[t] >= 0, name=f"NonNegSpaceSugar_{t}")
    model.addConstr(BuyGrain[t] >= 0, name=f"NonNegBuyGrain_{t}")
    model.addConstr(BuySugar[t] >= 0, name=f"NonNegBuySugar_{t}")
    model.addConstr(SellGrain[t] >= 0, name=f"NonNegSellGrain_{t}")
    model.addConstr(SellSugar[t] >= 0, name=f"NonNegSellSugar_{t}")
    model.addConstr(ExtraLabour[t] >= 0, name=f"NonNegExtraLabour_{t}")

constraints.description: The total number of dairy cows at end of five years must not decrease by more than 50% or increase by more than 75% from the initial number

code.gurobipy:
model.addConstr(NumCows[4] >= 0.5 * NMilk, name="MinFinalCows")
model.addConstr(NumCows[4] <= 1.75 * NMilk, name="MaxFinalCows")

objective.description: Maximize the farmer's profit over the next five years
objective.formulation: \max \ \sum_{t=1}^{5} \left( RevenueMilk \times NumCows_{t} + PriceBullock \times 0.5 \times CowCalve \times NumCows_{t} + PriceHeifer \times SellHeifer_{t} + SellPriceGrain \times SellGrain_{t} + SellPriceSugar \times SellSugar_{t} - BuyPriceGrain \times BuyGrain_{t} - BuyPriceSugar \times BuySugar_{t} - ExtraHeifers \times NumHeifers_{t} - ExtraCow \times NumCows_{t} - ExtraGrain \times SpaceGrainUsed_{t} - ExtraSugar \times SpaceSugar_{t} - LabourCost - ExtraLabourCost \times ExtraLabour_{t} - CowOutlay \times NumExtraCow_{t} \right)

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(gurobipy.quicksum(RevenueMilk * NumCows[t] + PriceBullock * 0.5 * CowCalve * NumCows[t] + PriceHeifer * SellHeifer[t] + SellPriceGrain * SellGrain[t] + SellPriceSugar * SellSugar[t] - BuyPriceGrain * BuyGrain[t] - BuyPriceSugar * BuySugar[t] - ExtraHeifers * NumHeifers[t] - ExtraCow * NumCows[t] - ExtraGrain * SpaceGrainUsed[t] - ExtraSugar * SpaceSugar[t] - LabourCost - ExtraLabourCost * ExtraLabour[t] - CowOutlay * NumExtraCow[t] for t in range(5)), GRB.MAXIMIZE)

problem_info.parametrized_description: Maximize total productive capacity at the end of the five years. The problem parameters are a matrix of coefficients InputOne, a vector of manpower coefficients for input one ManpowerOne, a matrix of coefficients InputTwo, a vector of manpower coefficients for input two ManpowerTwo, initial stock levels Stock, productive capacities Capacity, a company-wide manpower limit ManpowerLimit, and demand for each productive unit Demand.

NumYears.description: Total number of years in the planning horizon

Stock.description: Initial stock level for each of the productive units

Capacity.description: Productive capacity for each productive unit

Demand.description: Demand for each of the productive units at the end of the planning horizon

Produce.description: Amount of units produced by industry k in year t

BuildCapa.description: Amount of units used to build productive capacity for industry k in year t

StockHold.description: Amount of stock of industry k held at end of year t

constraints.description: There is a company-wide manpower limit denoted by ManpowerLimit
constraints.formulation: \sum_{k=1}^{NumIndustries} ManpowerOne_{k} \times Produce_{k,t} + \sum_{k=1}^{NumIndustries} ManpowerTwo_{k} \times BuildCapa_{k,t} \leq ManpowerLimit, \quad \forall t \in \{1, \ldots, NumYears\}

code.gurobipy:
for t in range(NumYears):
    model.addConstr(quicksum(ManpowerOne[k] * Produce[k][t] for k in range(NumIndustries)) + quicksum(ManpowerTwo[k] * BuildCapa[k][t] for k in range(NumIndustries)) <= ManpowerLimit, name=f"ManpowerLimit_{t}")

constraints.description: The production must meet certain demand levels specified by Demand
constraints.formulation: Produce_{k,t} + StockHold_{k,t-1} - StockHold_{k,t} - \sum_{j=1}^{NumIndustries} InputOne_{j,k} \times Produce_{j,t} - \sum_{j=1}^{NumIndustries} InputTwo_{j,k} \times BuildCapa_{j,t} \geq Demand_{k}, \quad \forall k \in \{1, \ldots, NumIndustries\}, \forall t \in \{1, \ldots, NumYears\}

constraints.description: Production cannot exceed productive capacity, which increases based on past capacity building
constraints.formulation: Produce_{k,t} \leq Capacity_{k} + \sum_{t'=1}^{t-2} BuildCapa_{k,t'}, \quad \forall k \in \{1, \ldots, NumIndustries\}, \forall t \in \{1, \ldots, NumYears\}

constraints.description: Each input usage and productive capacity must be non-negative

objective.description: Maximize total productive capacity at the end of the five years

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(quicksum(Capacity[k] + quicksum(BuildCapa[k][t] for t in range(NumYears - 1)) for k in range(NumIndustries)), GRB.MAXIMIZE)

problem_info.parametrized_description: Maximize total production in the last two years using the given constraints on production inputs from two resource types (InputOne and InputTwo), manpower allocations (ManpowerOne and ManpowerTwo), stock levels (Stock), capacities (Capacity), and the upper limit of manpower (ManpowerLimit).

InputOne.description: The production inputs from resource type one to product k in year j

ManpowerOne.description: The manpower allocated to resource type one for product k

InputTwo.description: The production inputs from resource type two to product k in year j

ManpowerTwo.description: The manpower allocated to resource type two for product k

Stock.description: Stock levels of product k at the start of the planning horizon

Produce.description: Amount of units produced by industry k in year t

BuildCapa.description: Amount of units used to build productive capacity for industry k in year t

StockHold.description: Amount of stock of industry k held at end of year t

constraints.description: Material balance: output plus previous stock minus current stock minus inputs to production and capacity building must be non-negative
constraints.formulation: Produce_{k,t} + StockHold_{k,t-1} - StockHold_{k,t} - \sum_{j=1}^{NumIndustries} InputOne_{j,k} \times Produce_{j,t} - \sum_{j=1}^{NumIndustries} InputTwo_{j,k} \times BuildCapa_{j,t} \geq 0, \quad \forall k \in \{1, \ldots, NumIndustries\}, \forall t \in \{1, \ldots, NumYears\}

constraints.description: Production capacities must be respected according to Capacity
constraints.formulation: Produce_{k,t} \leq Capacity_{k} + \sum_{t'=1}^{t-2} BuildCapa_{k,t'}, \quad \forall k \in \{1, \ldots, NumIndustries\}, \forall t \in \{1, \ldots, NumYears\}

constraints.description: The total manpower used must not exceed the upper limit, ManpowerLimit
constraints.formulation: \sum_{k=1}^{NumIndustries} ManpowerOne_{k} \times Produce_{k,t} + \sum_{k=1}^{NumIndustries} ManpowerTwo_{k} \times BuildCapa_{k,t} \leq ManpowerLimit, \quad \forall t \in \{1, \ldots, NumYears\}

code.gurobipy:
for t in range(NumYears):
    model.addConstr(quicksum(ManpowerOne[k] * Produce[k][t] for k in range(NumIndustries)) + quicksum(ManpowerTwo[k] * BuildCapa[k][t] for k in range(NumIndustries)) <= ManpowerLimit, name=f"ManpowerLimit_{t}")

constraints.description: Stock levels must be maintained as per Stock non-negativity and limits
All variables of production, resources, and manpower must be non-negative
constraints.formulation: Produce_{k,t} \geq 0, \quad BuildCapa_{k,t} \geq 0, \quad \forall k \in \{1, \ldots, NumIndustries\}, \forall t \in \{1, \ldots, NumYears\}

objective.description: Maximize total production in the last two years

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(quicksum(Produce[k][t] for k in range(NumIndustries) for t in range(NumYears - 2, NumYears)), GRB.MAXIMIZE)

problem_info.parametrized_description: Maximize the total manpower requirement over five years. The manpower requirements for operations one in year k is ManpowerOne, and for operations two is ManpowerTwo. The stock level of product k at the beginning of the year is Stock, the production capacity for product k for the year is Capacity, and the demand for product k for the year is Demand. The input one coefficient for product k with respect to product j is InputOne, and the input two coefficient for product k with respect to product j is InputTwo.

K.description: The number of years over which the total manpower requirement is maximized

NumIndustries.description: The number of industries in the economy

ManpowerOne.description: Manpower requirements for operations one in each industry

ManpowerTwo.description: Manpower requirements for operations two in each industry

Stock.description: Stock level of product k at the beginning of the year

Capacity.description: Production capacity for product k for the year

Demand.description: Demand for product k for the year

InputOne.description: Input one coefficient for product k with respect to product j

InputTwo.description: Input two coefficient for product k with respect to product j

Produce.description: Amount of units produced by industry k in year t

BuildCapa.description: Amount of units used to build productive capacity for industry k in year t

StockHold.description: Amount of stock of industry k held at end of year t

constraints.description: Material balance: production plus previous stock minus current stock minus inputs to production and capacity building must satisfy demand
constraints.formulation: Produce_{k,t} + StockHold_{k,t-1} - StockHold_{k,t} - \sum_{j=1}^{NumIndustries} InputOne_{j,k} \times Produce_{j,t} - \sum_{j=1}^{NumIndustries} InputTwo_{j,k} \times BuildCapa_{j,t} \geq Demand_{k}, \quad \forall k \in \{1, \ldots, NumIndustries\}, \forall t \in \{1, \ldots, K\}

constraints.description: Production cannot exceed productive capacity, which increases based on past capacity building
constraints.formulation: Produce_{k,t} \leq Capacity_{k} + \sum_{t'=1}^{t-2} BuildCapa_{k,t'}, \quad \forall k \in \{1, \ldots, NumIndustries\}, \forall t \in \{1, \ldots, K\}

constraints.description: All production, capacity building, and stockholding variables must be non-negative
constraints.formulation: Produce_{k,t} \geq 0, \quad BuildCapa_{k,t} \geq 0, \quad StockHold_{k,t} \geq 0, \quad \forall k \in \{1, \ldots, NumIndustries\}, \forall t \in \{1, \ldots, K\}

objective.description: Maximize the total manpower requirement over five years

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(quicksum(ManpowerOne[k] * Produce[k][t] + ManpowerTwo[k] * BuildCapa[k][t] for k in range(NumIndustries) for t in range(K)), GRB.MAXIMIZE)

problem_info.parametrized_description: Where should each department be located so as to minimize overall yearly cost? The optimization should consider the benefit of department K being located at location L, the communication costs between departments K and K, and the costs of locating at location L with requirement L.

Benefit.description: Benefit of department k being located at location l

Communication.description: Communication costs between departments k and j

Cost.description: Cost of locating at location l with requirement m

IsLocated.description: Whether department k is located at city l (1 if located, 0 otherwise)

constraints.description: Each department must be located in exactly one city

code.gurobipy:
for k in range(K):
    model.addConstr(quicksum(IsLocated[k][l] for l in range(L)) == 1, name=f"OneLocation_{k}")

constraints.description: No city may be the location for more than three departments

code.gurobipy:
for l in range(L):
    model.addConstr(quicksum(IsLocated[k][l] for k in range(K)) <= 3, name=f"MaxDepts_{l}")

constraints.description: IsLocated variables are binary

objective.description: Minimize overall yearly cost, which is communication cost minus relocation benefits
objective.formulation: \min \sum_{k=1}^{K} \sum_{j=1}^{K} \sum_{l=1}^{L} \sum_{m=1}^{L} Communication_{k,j} \times Cost_{l,m} \times IsLocated_{k,l} \times IsLocated_{j,m} - \sum_{k=1}^{K} \sum_{l=1}^{L} Benefit_{k,l} \times IsLocated_{k,l}

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(quicksum(Communication[k][j] * Cost[l][m] * IsLocated[k][l] * IsLocated[j][m] for k in range(K) for j in range(K) for l in range(L) for m in range(L)) - quicksum(Benefit[k][l] * IsLocated[k][l] for k in range(K) for l in range(L)), GRB.MINIMIZE)

problem_info.parametrized_description: Fit the 'best' straight line Y = bX + a to this set of data points. The objective is to minimise the sum of absolute deviations of each observed value of Y from the value predicted by the linear relationship.

Y.description: Observed values of the dependent variable

X.description: Observed values of the independent variable

AbsDev.description: Absolute deviation of observed Y from predicted Y for data point k

constraints.description: The absolute deviation for each data point must be at least the positive deviation
constraints.formulation: AbsDev_{k} \geq (Slope \times X_{k} + Intercept) - Y_{k}, \quad \forall k \in \{1, \ldots, K\}

code.gurobipy:
for k in range(K):
    model.addConstr(AbsDev[k] >= (Slope * X[k] + Intercept) - Y[k], name=f"PosDev_{k}")

constraints.description: The absolute deviation for each data point must be at least the negative deviation
constraints.formulation: AbsDev_{k} \geq Y_{k} - (Slope \times X_{k} + Intercept), \quad \forall k \in \{1, \ldots, K\}

code.gurobipy:
for k in range(K):
    model.addConstr(AbsDev[k] >= Y[k] - (Slope * X[k] + Intercept), name=f"NegDev_{k}")

constraints.description: Absolute deviations must be non-negative

code.gurobipy:
for k in range(K):
    model.addConstr(AbsDev[k] >= 0, name=f"AbsDevNonNeg_{k}")

objective.description: Minimize the sum of absolute deviations between the observed Y values and the values predicted by the model Y = bX + a

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(quicksum(AbsDev[k] for k in range(K)), GRB.MINIMIZE)

problem_info.parametrized_description: Fit the ‘best’ straight line y = bx + a where the objective is to minimize the maximum deviation of all the K observed values of Y from the value predicted by the linear relationship. The observed values of y are given by Y and the observed values of x are given by X.
